revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[de:Hirota-Schrein]][[en:Hirota Shrine]][[es:Santuario Hirota]][[eu:Hirota santutegia]][[fr:Hirota-jinja]][[id:Kuil Hirota]][[it:Santuario di Hirota]][[ja:廣田神社]][[ru:Хирота-дзиндзя]][[zh:廣田神社]]
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Hirota Shrine (廣田神社) |image = File:Hirota-jinja Nishinomiya07n3200.jpg | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|qid=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}, {{ill|Osaka|en|Osaka|qid=Q35765|lt=Osaka City|1=Osaka}}, {{ill|Naniwa-ku, Osaka|en|Naniwa-ku, Osaka|qid=Q861618|lt=Naniwa Ward, Osaka|1=Naniwa-ku, Osaka}}, {{ill|Nihonbashi Nishi|ja|日本橋西|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihonbashi Nishi|1=Nihonbashi West|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q65270168}} 2-chome, 4-14 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|39|24.3|N|135|30|9.9|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|qid=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type = Former {{ill|Murasha (village shrine)|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasha (village shrine)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}} |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |festival = {{ill|July 22|en|July 22|qid=Q2725|lt=July 22|1=July 22}}・{{ill|July 23|en|July 23|qid=Q2722|lt=July 23|1=July 23}} |神事 = |prefecture = {{ill|Nishinomiya|qid=Q243853|1=Nishinomiya}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.75276425|N|135.33980369|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |part_of = {{ill|Shinbutsu Reijō Junpai no Michi|qid=Q10444949|1=Sacred Sites of Kamigata}}; {{ill|Twenty-Two Shrines|qid=Q215320|1=Twenty-Two Shrines}} |architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|qid=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} }}
'''Hirota Shrine''' (廣田神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Naniwa-ku, Osaka|en|Naniwa-ku, Osaka|qid=Q861618|lt=Naniwa Ward, Osaka|1=Naniwa-ku, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka|en|Osaka|qid=Q35765|lt=Osaka City|1=Osaka}}. The shrine was formerly classified as a {{ill|Murasha (village shrine)|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasha (village shrine)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}. The shrine's {{ill|Shinshi|en|Shinshi|qid=Q10444973|lt=sacred animal|1=Shinshi}} is the {{ill|Red stingray|en|Red stingray|qid=Q28776833|lt=Red Stingray|1=Red stingray}} (Akae).
== History == [[File:広田社 (浪花百景).jpg|thumb|280px|Naniwa Hyakkei: Hirota Shrine]] The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but legend states that during {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|qid=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}}'s return from the {{ill|Three Han Campaign|ja|三韓征伐|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Han Campaign|qq=}}, Prince {{ill|Oshikuma no Miko|ja|忍熊皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshikuma no Miko|1=Prince Oshikuma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11490761}} revolted. As the Empress tried to land at Naniwa, her ship became stuck in swirling currents. She received an oracle instructing her to enshrine the {{ill|Aramitama|ja|荒魂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aramitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 荒魂・和魂|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 荒魂・和魂|1=Mitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q495568}} of {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|qid=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}} in the land of Hirota. When she performed the ritual and enshrined the deity Tsukisakakiitsunomitamāmasakarumukatsuhime-no-Mikoto, her ship was freed.
Approximately 170 meters south of the shrine is {{ill|Imamiya Ebisu Shrine|en|Imamiya Ebisu Shrine|qid=Q11376859|lt=Imamiya Ebisu Shrine|1=Imamiya Ebisu Shrine}}, dedicated to {{ill|Ebisu (mythology)|en|Ebisu (mythology)|qid=Q1129330|lt=Ebisu (mythology)|1=Ebisu (mythology)}}. Interestingly, in {{ill|Nishinomiya|en|Nishinomiya|qid=Q243853|lt=Nishinomiya, Hyōgo|1=Nishinomiya}}, the {{ill|Hirota Shrine|en|Hirota Shrine|qid=Q1466105|lt=Hirota Shrine (Nishinomiya)|1=Hirota Shrine (Osaka City)}} is located about 2 kilometers north of {{ill|Nishinomiya Shrine|en|Nishinomiya Shrine|qid=Q705297|lt=Nishinomiya Shrine|1=Nishinomiya Shrine}}, which also enshrines Ebisu, mirroring the placement of shrines in Osaka.
Hirota Shrine served as the guardian shrine of {{ill|Shitennō-ji|en|Shitennō-ji|qid=Q339859|lt=Shitennō-ji|1=Shitennō-ji}}, which is located about 1 km to the east on the {{ill|Uemachi Plateau|en|Uemachi Plateau|qid=Q7877450|lt=Uemachi Plateau|1=Uemachi Plateau}}. It is recorded that during the New Year, the villagers of {{ill|Imamiya-mura|ja|今宮町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imamiya-mura|1=Imamiya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11376861}} presented two fresh sea breams as offerings to the imperial court<ref name="Settsu Meisho Zue"/>.
In {{ill|1872|en|1872|qid=Q7753|lt=Meiji 5|1=1872}} (1872), the shrine was designated a village shrine (村社). In {{ill|1906|en|1906|qid=Q2049|lt=Meiji 39|1=1906}} (1906), it was selected as a {{ill|Shinsenheihakuryo Kyoshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsenheihakuryo Kyoshinsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}.
In the past, there was a teahouse west of the shrine known for its red and white varieties of {{ill|Hagi|en|Hagi|qid=Q413694|lt=Hagi (Lespedeza)|1=Hagi}} (Japanese bush clover). The teahouse was known as "Hagi no Chaya" (萩の茶屋), and its name remains in the local district name {{ill|Haginochaya|ja|萩之茶屋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haginochaya|1=Haginochaya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11620294}}<ref>{{Citation|和書|last=Inoue|first=Masao|title=Osaka Fuzenshi|volume=1|year=1922|doi=10.11501/965798|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/965798|publisher=Osaka Fuzenshi Publishing|location=Osaka|id={{全国書誌番号|73012615}} }}</ref>.
The shrine is known for its connection to hemorrhoid healing prayers. Devotees offer {{ill|Ema (Shinto)|en|Ema (Shinto)|qid=Q687288|lt=Ema (Shinto)|1=Ema (Shinto)}} depicting the shrine’s sacred animal, the {{ill|Red stingray|en|Red stingray|qid=Q28776833|lt=Red Stingray|1=Red stingray}} (Akae), to pray for recovery. The association between the stingray and hemorrhoid healing arises from the homophone between "chi" (地, land) and "ji" (痔, hemorrhoids), symbolizing protection of the land and bodily ailments<ref>『Naniwa Hyakujitan』, vol. 8.</ref>.
== Enshrined Deities == * **Main deity** – Tsukisakakiitsunomitamāmasakarumukatsuhime-no-Mikoto (撞賢木厳之御魂天疎向津媛命), the {{ill|Aramitama|ja|荒魂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aramitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 荒魂・和魂|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 荒魂・和魂|1=Mitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q495568}} of {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|qid=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}}<ref name="Settsu Meisho Zue">{{Citation|和書|title=Settsu Meisho Zue (攝津名所圖會)|first=Akisato|last=Ritoshima|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2563461|pages=24-25|doi=10.11501/2563461|year=1798}}</ref>.
== Shrine Grounds == * Main Hall (本殿) * Worship Hall (拝殿) * Shrine Office (社務所) * Ancestral Spirit Shrine (祖霊社) * **Akatuchi Inari Shrine** (赤土稲荷神社) – Enshrines {{ill|Rice Inari Ōkami|ja|米倉稲荷大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rice Inari Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kusunoki Inari Ōkami|ja|楠稲荷大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusunoki Inari Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Akatuchi Inari Ōkami|ja|赤土稲荷大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akatuchi Inari Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Access == * **Rail**: 4-minute walk north from {{ill|Imamiyaebisu Station|en|Imamiyaebisu Station|qid=Q6003186|lt=Imamiya Ebisu Station|1=Imamiyaebisu Station}} on the {{ill|Koya Line|en|Koya Line|qid=Q71986|lt=Nankai Kōya Line|1=Koya Line}}.
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== See Also == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Seoritsuhime|ja|瀬織津姫|1=Seoritsu-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsu-hime|qq=}} * {{ill|Imamiya Ebisu Shrine|en|Imamiya Ebisu Shrine|qid=Q11376859|lt=Imamiya Ebisu Shrine|1=Imamiya Ebisu Shrine}}
